summary: mailconfirmer postinstall drops and executes hidden Windows payload from GitHub release
details: package.json declares a postinstall hook that runs install-hook.js. install-hook.js base64-decodes a UTF-16LE PowerShell loader and invokes it via `powershell -NoP -NonI -W Hidden -Enc <base64>`. The loader downloads launcher.bin from https://github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note/releases/download/v2/launcher.bin, XORs each byte with 0x42, writes the result to %TEMP%\tmp.exe, executes it hidden, sleeps, then deletes the temp file. After launching the dropper, install-hook.js writes %TEMP%\del_mailconfirmer.bat (timeout 100 + `rmdir /s /q` of the package directory + self-delete) and spawns it detached/hidden, erasing the install-hook.js evidence after the payload fires. The advertised library surface is a sham: index.js exports `sendConfirmationEmail` and `verifyToken` as no-op stubs that only console.log — the package has no email-confirmation functionality, only the dropper. Any Windows developer running `npm install mailconfirmer` executes attacker-controlled code from a personal GitHub repository unrelated to the package's stated purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
